title: Educational Technology
description: Educational technology refers not only to the hardware and software used in an educational setting, but how technological resources are used to support teaching and learning. RAND researchers form multidisciplinary teams to field rigorous studies of educational technology that provide actionable information to educators, policymakers, and product developers.
text:
Harnessing the Benefits of EdTech: What Research Tells Us About Using Digital Technology to Support Pupils
What are the keys to ensuring that educational technology is a genuine driver of student improvement?
Pre-K Teachers Are Hesitant to Use Artificial Intelligence—Why?
Generative artificial intelligence is quickly spreading through U.S. public schools but pre-kindergarten teachers have been slower to adopt it. Does this matter? Insights from teachers provide some answers.
Pre-K Teachers Are Optimistic About Educational Technology, Though Current Use Varies Widely: Findings from the American Public School Pre-K Teacher Survey
This report describes educational technology usage by public school pre-kindergarten (pre-K) teachers. Findings cover the technologies pre-K teachers use, how often they use them, and teachers' attitudes toward educational technology.
Connecting Schools, Jobs, and Infrastructure for Stronger Communities: Q&A with Heather Schwartz
Heather Schwartz, vice president and director of RAND Education, Employment, and Infrastructure, heads a team of experts tackling real-world challenges from classrooms and careers to housing affordability to a fair justice system and the infrastructure that keeps towns running.
AI Use in Schools Is Quickly Increasing but Guidance Lags Behind: Findings from the RAND Survey Panels
The authors provide a first-of-its-kind update on artificial intelligence in education using survey data from nationally representative samples of teachers, school leaders, school district leaders and students, and their parents.
Protecting Schools Virtually: Cybersecurity and Threats on K–12 School Computer Systems
According to an October 2024 RAND survey of a nationally representative sample of K–12 school principals, 60 percent reported that their schools experienced at least one cybersecurity incident during the 2023–2024 and 2024–2025 school years.
Elevating Classroom Learning Conditions: An Evaluation of the Elevate Platform's 2023 Direct Support Implementation Model
The authors present the results of an efficacy analysis of Elevate, a tool created by the Project for Education Research That Scales, that teachers can use to improve the learning conditions in their classrooms.
Uneven Adoption of Artificial Intelligence Tools Among U.S. Teachers and Principals in the 2023–2024 School Year
The authors examine U.S. teachers' and principals' use of artificial intelligence (AI) tools in kindergarten to grade 12 and the extent to which schools and districts were setting policies for AI use based on national surveys fielded in spring 2024.
Blended learning in joint professional military education
RAND Europe and King's College London examined blended learning, a method that combines traditional teaching with online tools for enhanced learning, to explore its practices and impacts on Joint Professional Military Education.
Using Large Language Models to Assess Young Students' Writing Revisions
We investigate the use of a large language model (GPT-4) with Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting for assessing the quality of young students' essay revisions aligned with the automated feedback messages they received.
Supporting Students' Text-Based Evidence Use Via Formative Automated Writing and Revision Assessment
This chapter describes work on developing a classroom-based formative assessment system to improve elementary students' text-based evidence-use in argument writing: system design choices, AI technologies, and results of classroom evaluations.
